mnemonics.title: Installation Sequence记忆法
mnemonics.content: SHEep GIVE BIRTH: Site prep → Hardware → Environment setup → Software → Go-Live → Integration → Baseline → Burn-in → Acceptance → Rollback ready → Testing → Handover

mnemonics.title: Testing Types记忆法
mnemonics.content: FAT SAT: Factory Accept before Shipping, Site Accept after Transfer - FAT at vendor, SAT at customer site

acronyms.usage: Testing done at vendor's premises before delivery to verify software meets specifications
Testing done at customer site after installation to validate functionality in actual environment
The moment when installed system starts handling real business workloads
Alternate term for SAT, validating the entire system meets user requirements
Repository storing configuration items and their relationships for tracking changes

visualTips.visualization: Draw a flowchart: Site Prep → Hardware Install → Software Install → Testing → Go-Live. Each phase feeds into the next, with feedback loops for issues.
Create a two-column table: FAT on left (vendor premises, before delivery), SAT on right (customer site, after installation). Both verify requirements but at different stages.

visualTips.visualize: Show two side-by-side systems - Old System (left, labeled 'Active') and New System (right, labeled 'Validating'). Arrow showing gradual transition from old to new over time.

analogies.analogy: Like moving to a new house - you unpack, set up utilities, test everything works before declaring 'we live here now'.
Like test-driving a new car while still owning the old one - you use both for a while to ensure the new one is reliable.
Like having a spare parachute - you hope never to use it, but having it ready makes the jump less risky.
Like a snag list when moving into a new house - items that need fixing before you fully accept the property.
Like taking a photo of your room setup - if you change things, you have a reference to return to.
